post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 232
Unpaid Intern Central Invitational I


For want of a nail the shoe was lost.
For want of a shoe the horse was lost.
For want of a horse the rider was lost.
For want of a rider the message was lost.
For want of a message the battle was lost.
For want of a battle the kingdom was lost.
And all for the want of a horseshoe nail.

- Important Rules -
This tab summarizes the most important rules.

If you have time, familiarizing yourself with the rest of the rules may be helpful, but this is the only tab you need to read.

Integrity
  • Do not edit or delete your posts, even for typos. Just post again.
  • Do not OOT (Out of Thread) and talk about this game outside your role PM, QTs, approved Discord channels, or this thread, even if you are dead. Access to the spectator chats will be granted upon request.
  • Do not gamethrow or intentionally sabotage your wincondition.
  • Do not attach files or link to external documents concerning this game. Discussions must stay in this thread.
  • Do not use OGI (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games.

Activity
  • Each "day" is 2 weeks while each "night" is 2 days.
  • Do not post during night.
  • This game uses Dawn start; Day 1 will not start until everyone shows that they have read the rules.
  • All times will be in Pacific Standard Time (PST, UTC-8) or Pacific Daylight Time (PDT, UTC-7) as applicable.
  • Activity rules are 48 hours to in-game prod and 72 hours to PM prod.

Voting
  • You must format your votes correctly. Capitalization doesn't matter, and capital i/lowercase L are not differentiated.
  • Majority hang is off for Day 1, but it is on starting Day 2.
  • Deadline hang is on from Day 1 but it is off for LYLO.
  • Votes are locked at LYLO.

Etiquette and Warnings
  • Use common sense.
  • Do not use formatting that makes text hard to read.
  • You may attack a player’s opinion, but do not make personal attacks on players themselves.
  • You can receive up to 2 warnings before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill.
  • All other rules are standard. If you break a rule that isn't covered here, you may still be warned.

Setup and Moderators
  • This game is based on the High Noon setup.
  • Mafia have access to daychat.
  • All role mechanics and Night Action Resolutions are standard.
  • This game is moderated by Nike100 and ad333.
  • This game operates optional discord spectator and mafia private chats using the Unpaid Intern Central server. PM the mods to request access.

Resources and Support
  • For any issues, you may contact us through your Role PM or through Discord (Nike#9191/This is just like 1984#8451).
  • For a list of basic resources, please refer to the Resources tab below.
  • For an more extensive list of resources compiled by the UIMT, please go here.
  • If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or help you find a better way to process events. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer; you can ask us about this in your role PM.
  • Congratulations, you made it through the summary tab! Post your favorite emoji or emoticon to show you've made it this far.

  • General
    Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas, among others, as well as the Mafia Universe forum rules, for the rule template.

    The following are standard mafia game rules.

    • Do not post during the night phase.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderators. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
    • Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent].
    • Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON.
    • If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate, and access to the spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered.
    • Do not impersonate the mods.
    • Do not lie to the mods.
    • Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.

  • Votes and Hangs
    This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.

    • Votes must be in this format: Vote: [player username] to be counted.
    • Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly. Do not use nicknames.
    • For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical.
    • For example, both Vote: mathjay and Vote: mAtHjAy are valid votes, but vote: mathjay, Vote: jay, Vote: mathjay., Vote:mathjay, Vote: mathjay, and Vote: mathjay are invalid.
    • Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is bolded, even with separate bold tags, are invalid.
    • Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
    • Vote to hang no one by posting Vote: No Hang.
    • Unvote by posting Unvote. You do not have to unvote before voting again.
    • Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Please consider notifying the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
    • Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
    • This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
    • If two or more players have the same number of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If no votes have been cast by the deadline, no one will be hanged.
    • Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
    • Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
    • Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team.
    • The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
    • Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.
  • Activity
    This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.

    • All times will be in Pacific Standard Time (PST, UTC-8) or Pacific Daylight Time (PDT, UTC-7) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
    • Each day phase will be 336 hours (14 days), and each night phase will be 48 hours (2 days).
    • The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
    • If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform a NA that night.
    • Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
    • Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night. You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates in the case of a missed deadline.
    • Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in bold in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded.
    • V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for more options.
    • Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting Request Replacement in-thread or in their role PM.
    • Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in.
    • Players will receive an in-game prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Players will receive a PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA.
    • Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 108 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
    • If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
    • If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
    • Activity will be reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart.
    • If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
    • If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
  • Integrity and Etiquette
    This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.

    • Do not gamethrow.
    • Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other interactions with moderators.
    • Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
    • Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably think as alignment indicative.

    The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, please click here.

    • Do not make personal attacks.
    • Do not spam.
    • Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
    • Follow AoPS Community Guidelines.
    • Be mature and use common sense.
    • For minor rule infractions, all other players can receive up to two warnings. It is possible to receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.

    The above rules broadly cover most etiquette issues, but for specific details and examples of inconsiderate gameplay, please click here.

  • Moderation Policies
    This tab codifies several moderation procedures.

    • Role Randomization

    When randomizing roles for the game, the moderators will not “reroll” or otherwise engage in intentional RNG manipulation.

    Current RNG Process (High Noon)
    Using the Google Random Number Generator two numbers were generated out of 1-9 to be assigned Mafia Goon. The rest were assigned VT.


    • Tiebreaking

    If there is a tie at deadline, the player on whom the last vote was made first will be hanged. For example, if the most recent vote on mathjay was made in Post #2106 and the most recent vote on HannahW was made in Post #2132, mathjay will be hanged in the case of a tie. This avoids tiebreaking via RNG while preventing hang manipulation by a single player unvoting and revoting.

    • Votes cast during deadline

    If votes or unvotes are cast near the EOD deadline, the moderators will reference the officially logged post times. These can be found by locating the relevant post at aops.com/community/u[player ID] and referencing its upper-right corner.

    Votes posted on or after the deadline will not be counted. For example, if the deadline is at 2:00 PM, votes logged as being posted at 2:00 and 2:01 will not count.

    • Mafia Night Kill Disagreement

    The moderators encourage the Mafia members to discuss the night kill using the private chat and agree upon a kill. If the players cannot arrive at a conclusion by the night deadline and continue to be in disagreement, however, the mafia factional kill will not go through. The moderators will not arbitrate this disagreement.

    • Game-advancing Posts

    A "game-advancing post" is anything other than an activity post, ie. a post solely made for the purposes of dodging a prod. Most posts other than "activity post" or "hi i got prodded bye" will be counted in activity checks.

    • Postbans

    Due to clarifications by the AoPS Sheriff, moderators cannot proxy posts from postbanned players. If a player is postbanned, they will be replaced. This may be subject to negotiation if the postban is less than a week in length.

    Please note that we are only prohibited from proxying posts from a postbanned player. If you have trouble accessing AoPS for any other reason, we will do our best to help.

    You may contact the moderators through discord at Nike#9191 or by editing posts in your role PM if you are unable to post on AoPS.

    • Outside Consulting

    If needed, moderators may contact a forum or other respected mod for help resolving an incident that could seriously impact game integrity. If you are ever involved in such an incident and disagree with the moderators' course of action, you may request the moderators to consult a second opinion, if they have not done so already.

    • Out Of Thread

    Due to clarifications by fdas, strictly mentioning procedural aspects of this game, such as game number, rules, player names, will not be OOT. However anything beyond that, such as gamestate, gameplays, will be OOT.(excluding flavor!)

  • Moderator Information
    This tab provides technical information about this game.

    • Nike100 and ad333 are the moderators of Mafia Game 232.
    • Flavor has been written mostly by the participants.
    • This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access to the server as appropriate.
    • Red text(#ff4d4d) will be used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
    • This color(#55d11f) will be used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators.
    • Green text is generally used for town flavor-related text.
    • Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes.
    • Current custom shades are: Town flavor green (#00f752), Activity Check blue (#005dff), mathjay's blue (#4a86e8), HannahW's blue (#0b5394), Mathletesv's blue (#4d94ff), Scipio1's blue (#3d70ff), Socoobo's orange (#ff8000).
    • The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History.
    • Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules.
    • Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in OCRSTM Version History.
    • The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game.

    MG232 OP is based on Overcomplicated RulesetTM (OCRSTM) version 8.
    Current version: 8.4
    Version History
Setup
This game is based on the High Noon setup. This is a open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players, comprised of 7 Town players and 2 Mafia players. There are no PRs in this game, and everyone is either a Vanilla Townie or Mafia Goon. However, all players have the ability to challenge another player to a Duel by posting Duel: [username]. Once a duel has been started, all non-duelists must vote between the dueling players, and the player who reaches majority will be killed.

There are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified:
  • A maximum of 1 Duel can be held each day.
  • Duels will not extend the EOD deadline.
  • If the deadline is reached while a Duel is in progress, the outcome of the Duel will be randomized and the day with end with a No Hang.
  • A duel will only be decided once a strict majority of non-duelists vote for one of the duelists. In the case of an even number of non-duelists, a tie will not end the Duel.
  • Votecounts will be reset after each Duel.
  • Mafia have Daychat.

Role PMs
Town Role PM wrote:
Wake up.
What are your desires?
You should work together.
Who do you want to be?
Everything will be fine.
What is your purpose?
Trust your team.
How do you want to die?
Trust. Your. Team.

Welcome to our world, [player]. Only seven of the nine people you see have good intentions; two of them want you dead. Your goal is to find them before it’s too late. Like everyone else, you can vote, or challenge anyone to a Duel during the day.

Now go to sleep.
Show them strength in the face of adversity.
You won’t remember this when you wake up.
Mafia Role PM wrote:
Wake up.
What are your desires?
You should kill.
Who do you want to be?
Sow distrust.
What is your purpose?
Make this a nightmare.
How do you want to die?
Never let them forget.

Welcome to our world, [player]. Your goal is to kill everyone until you and your partner [player] are the only ones left. The two of you can communicate without anyone else knowing here, and you can choose a player to kill each night. Like everyone else, you can vote, or challenge anyone to a Duel during the day.

Now go to sleep.
Show them humanity at its worst.
You won’t remember this when you wake up.

Modlog

May 19, 2021, 21:52 - Edits started for MG232 UIC Invitational based on OCRSTM 8.2

Automatic ISO Code
Written by casi and improved by Mathletesv. Reposted with permission.

ISO Code
Number.prototype.between = function(a, b, inclusive) {
  var min = Math.min(a, b),
    max = Math.max(a, b);
min -=1, max += 1;
 
  return inclusive ? this >= min && this <= max : this > min && this < max;
}
function number_matches(quote_box,start,end) {
return (parseInt($(quote_box).parents(".cmty-post-tr").children(".cmty-post-middle").children(".cmty-post-top-inline.cmty-post-top-data").children(".cmty-post-top").children(".cmty-post-number").text().slice(1)).between(start,end) )
    }
function user_matches (quote_box,username) {
    return ($(quote_box).parent().parent().children(".cmty-post-left").children(".cmty-post-username").text() == username)
    }

user = prompt("What is the username of the person you are iso'ing?");
min = parseInt(prompt("First number of range"), 10);
max = parseInt(prompt("Second number of range"), 10);
$(".cmty-post-quote").each(function () {
    if (user_matches(this, user) && number_matches(this, min, max)) {
        $(this).click()
    }
    })


Source


Description: given a username and a range of posts to look in, this code will automatically quote all posts from the specified player into the reply field.

Note: this code does not work on Android, iOS, or any other device without a javascript web console.

Instructions
Step 1: Load the posts you want to ISO.
The code cannot retrieve a player’s posts if you do not have them currently loaded in your browser, so you must scroll down (or up) through the thread to load the posts you want in your ISO. AoPS periodically refuses to load posts when scrolling up; if this happens, “bounce” the thread by scrolling down for a bit before scrolling back up.
Step 2: Open your browser’s console.
For Chrome, the shortcut is Control + Shift + J (Windows, Linux, Chrome OS) or Command + Option + J (macOS). For Safari, first enable Developer Menu by going to Safari Menu > Preferences > Advanced tab and checking “Show Develop menu in menu bar,” then use the shortcut Command + Option + C. For Firefox, the shortcut is Control + Shift + K (Windows, Linux) or Command + Option + K (macOS). For Microsoft Edge, the shortcut is Control + Shift + J.
Step 3: Copy/paste the code into the console and hit Enter.
Once you do so, the code will prompt you in a popup, and you will not be able to access the tab you are running the code on. Do not switch to another tab in the same window, or the code will not work.
Step 4: Enter the username of the player you are ISOing.
This is case-sensitive; capitalize and spell the username correctly.
Step 5: Enter the first post of your ISO range.
If you are doing a full ISO, you can use 1 or 0. If you are doing a partial ISO, enter the post number from which you want the code to search for a player’s posts.
Step 6: Enter the last post of your ISO range.
If you are doing a full ISO, enter any number larger than the thread postcount; 10000 works. If you are doing a partial ISO, enter the post number to which you want the code to search for a player’s posts. Please note that posts that are within the range you have specified, but that have not been loaded in your browser, will not be included by the code in the ISO.

Once you have done so, the reply field will open, with all the relevant posts quoted in it.


Important notes:
  • The username is case sensitive.
  • The min/max limits of the range do not have to be exact.
  • The first range input must be smaller than the second range input.
  • This code does not work in [pywindow].
  • You cannot access the contents of the tab you are running the code in while it is running, so remember to memorize the target username before running the code.
  • You can rerun the code once it has finished running.
  • You can run the code even if you have a reply partly written.
  • If the code refuses to work, check that all of the above has been done correctly. If it still does not work, reload the tab and try again.

For further troubleshooting, please PM the moderators of the game you are in.


Game is currently in progress. If you are not playing in this game, please do not post. You can go to this thread to sign up.

Current phase: The game is over, Town has won.

[*]For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical. 
[*]For example, both [b]Vote: mathjay[/b] and [b]Vote: mAtHjAy[/b] are valid votes, but [b]vote: mathjay[/b], [b]Vote: jay[/b], [b]Vote: mathjay.[/b], [b]Vote:mathjay[/b], Vote: [b]mathjay[/b], and [b][i]Vote: mathjay[/i][/b] are invalid. 
[*]Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is bolded, even with separate bold tags, are invalid. 
[*]Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
[*]Vote to hang no one by posting [b]Vote: No Hang[/b].
[*]Unvote by posting [b]Unvote[/b]. You do not have to unvote before voting again. 
[*]Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Please consider notifying the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
[*]Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
[*]This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged. 
[*]If two or more players have the same number of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If no votes have been cast by the deadline, no one will be hanged. 
[*]Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
[*]Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
[*]Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team. 
[*]The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
[*]Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.[/list][/hide]
[*][hide=Activity]
[b]This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.[/b]


[list][*]All times will be in Pacific Standard Time (PST, UTC-8) or Pacific Daylight Time (PDT, UTC-7) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
[*]Each day phase will be 336 hours (14 days), and each night phase will be 48 hours (2 days). 
[*]The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
[*]If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform a NA that night.
[*]Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
[*]Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night. You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates in the case of a missed deadline. 
[*]Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in [b]bold[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded. 
[*]V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for more options. 
[*]Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting [b]Request Replacement[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. 
[*]Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in. 
[*]Players will receive an in-game prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
[*]Players will receive a PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA. 
[*]Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 108 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement. 
[*]If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
[*]If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
[*]Activity will be reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart. 
[*]If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
[*]If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.[list][/hide]
[*][hide=Integrity and Etiquette]
[b]This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.[/b]


[list][*]Do not gamethrow. 
[*]Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other interactions with moderators. 
[*]Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
[*]Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably think as alignment indicative. [/list]

The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, [url=https://aops.com/community/p21335214]please click here.[/url]


[list][*]Do not make personal attacks.
[*]Do not spam. 
[*]Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning. 
[*]Follow AoPS Community Guidelines. 
[*]Be mature and use common sense. 
[*]For minor rule infractions, all other players can receive up to two warnings. It is possible to receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.[/list]

The above rules broadly cover most etiquette issues, but for specific details and examples of inconsiderate gameplay, [url=https://aops.com/community/p21335246]please click here.[/url][/hide]

[*][hide=Moderation Policies]
[b]This tab codifies several moderation procedures.[/b]


[list][*]Role Randomization[/list]

When randomizing roles for the game, the moderators will not “reroll” or otherwise engage in intentional RNG manipulation. 

[hide=Current RNG Process (High Noon)]
Using the Google Random Number Generator two numbers were generated out of 1-9 to be assigned Mafia Goon. The rest were assigned VT.[/hide]


[list][*]Tiebreaking[/list]

If there is a tie at deadline, the player on whom the last vote was made first will be hanged. For example, if the most recent vote on mathjay was made in Post #2106 and the most recent vote on HannahW was made in Post #2132, mathjay will be hanged in the case of a tie. This avoids tiebreaking via RNG while preventing hang manipulation by a single player unvoting and revoting. 


[list][*]Votes cast during deadline[/list]

If votes or unvotes are cast near the EOD deadline, the moderators will reference the officially logged post times. These can be found by locating the relevant post at aops.com/community/u[player ID] and referencing its upper-right corner. 

Votes posted on or after the deadline will not be counted. For example, if the deadline is at 2:00 PM, votes logged as being posted at 2:00 and 2:01 will not count. 


[list][*]Mafia Night Kill Disagreement[/list]

The moderators encourage the Mafia members to discuss the night kill using the private chat and agree upon a kill. If the players cannot arrive at a conclusion by the night deadline and continue to be in disagreement, however, the mafia factional kill will not go through. The moderators will not arbitrate this disagreement. 


[list][*]Game-advancing Posts[/list]

A "game-advancing post" is anything other than an activity post, ie. a post solely made for the purposes of dodging a prod. Most posts other than "activity post" or "hi i got prodded bye" will be counted in activity checks. 


[list][*]Postbans[/list]

Due to clarifications by the AoPS Sheriff, moderators cannot proxy posts from postbanned players. If a player is postbanned, they will be replaced. This may be subject to negotiation if the postban is less than a week in length. 

Please note that we are only prohibited from proxying posts from a postbanned player. If you have trouble accessing AoPS for any other reason, we will do our best to help. 

You may contact the moderators through discord at [b]Nike#9191[/b] or by editing posts in your role PM if you are unable to post on AoPS. 


[list][*]Outside Consulting[/list]

If needed, moderators may contact a forum or other respected mod for help resolving an incident that could seriously impact game integrity. If you are ever involved in such an incident and disagree with the moderators' course of action, you may request the moderators to consult a second opinion, if they have not done so already.


[list][*]Out Of Thread[/list]

Due to [url=http://bit.ly/fmooc]clarifications[/url] by fdas, strictly mentioning procedural aspects of this game, such as game number, rules, player names, will not be OOT. However anything beyond that, such as gamestate, gameplays, will be OOT.(excluding flavor!)
[/hide]

[*][hide=Moderator Information]
[b]This tab provides technical information about this game.[/b]


[list][*]Nike100 and ad333 are the moderators of Mafia Game 232. 
[*]Flavor has been written mostly by the participants. 
[*]This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access to the server as appropriate. 
[*][color=#ff4d4d]Red text(#ff4d4d)[/color] will be used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
[*][color=#55d11f]This color(#55d11f)[/color] will be used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators. 
[*][color=#00FF00]Green text[/color] is generally used for town flavor-related text.
[*]Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes. 
[*]Current custom shades are: Town flavor green ([color=#00f752]#00f752[/color]), Activity Check blue ([color=#005dff]#005dff[/color]), mathjay's blue ([color=#4a86e8]#4a86e8[/color]), HannahW's blue ([color=#0b5394]#0b5394[/color]), Mathletesv's blue ([color=#4d94ff]#4d94ff[/color]), Scipio1's blue ([color=#3d70ff]#3d70ff[/color]), Socoobo's orange ([color=#ff8000]#ff8000[/color]).
[*]The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
[*]If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect votecount will become official.
[*]The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules. 
[*]Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game. [/list]

[b]MG232 OP is based on Overcomplicated Ruleset[sup]TM[/sup] (OCRSTM) version 8.[/b]
[i]Current version: 8.4[/i]
[url=https://aops.com/community/p21335350]Version History[/url]
[/hide][/list]
[hide=Setup]This game is based on the [url=https://wiki.mafiascum.net/index.php?title=High_Noon]High Noon[/url] setup. This is a open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players, comprised of 7 Town players and 2 Mafia players. There are no PRs in this game, and everyone is either a Vanilla Townie or Mafia Goon. However, all players have the ability to challenge another player to a Duel by posting [b]Duel: [username][/b]. Once a duel has been started, all non-duelists must vote between the dueling players, and the player who reaches majority will be killed. 

There are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified: 
[list]
[*]A maximum of 1 Duel can be held each day. 
[*]Duels will not extend the EOD deadline. 
[*]If the deadline is reached while a Duel is in progress, the outcome of the Duel will be randomized and the day with end with a No Hang. 
[*]A duel will only be decided once a strict majority of non-duelists vote for one of the duelists. In the case of an even number of non-duelists, a tie will not end the Duel. 
[*]Votecounts will be reset after each Duel. 
[*]Mafia have Daychat. [/list]
[/list]

[hide=Automatic ISO Code][b]Written by [url=https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/user/225381]casi[/url] and improved by [url=aops.com/community/user/417841]Mathletesv[/url]. Reposted with permission.[/b]

[hide=ISO Code][code]
Number.prototype.between = function(a, b, inclusive) {
  var min = Math.min(a, b),
    max = Math.max(a, b);
min -=1, max += 1;
 
  return inclusive ? this >= min && this <= max : this > min && this < max;
}
function number_matches(quote_box,start,end) {
return (parseInt($(quote_box).parents(".cmty-post-tr").children(".cmty-post-middle").children(".cmty-post-top-inline.cmty-post-top-data").children(".cmty-post-top").children(".cmty-post-number").text().slice(1)).between(start,end) )
    }
function user_matches (quote_box,username) {
    return ($(quote_box).parent().parent().children(".cmty-post-left").children(".cmty-post-username").text() == username)
    }

user = prompt("What is the username of the person you are iso'ing?");
min = parseInt(prompt("First number of range"), 10);
max = parseInt(prompt("Second number of range"), 10);
$(".cmty-post-quote").each(function () {
    if (user_matches(this, user) && number_matches(this, min, max)) {
        $(this).click()
    }
    })
[/code]

[url=aops.com/community/p16738769]Source[/url][/hide]

[b]Description:[/b] given a username and a range of posts to look in, this code will automatically quote all posts from the specified player into the reply field. 

[i]Note: this code does not work on Android, iOS, or any other device without a javascript web console.[/i]

[hide=Instructions]
[b]Step 1: Load the posts you want to ISO.[/b]
The code cannot retrieve a player’s posts if you do not have them currently loaded in your browser, so you must scroll down (or up) through the thread to load the posts you want in your ISO. AoPS periodically refuses to load posts when scrolling up; if this happens, “bounce” the thread by scrolling down for a bit before scrolling back up. 
[b]Step 2: Open your browser’s console. [/b]
For Chrome, the shortcut is [b]Control + Shift + J[/b] (Windows, Linux, Chrome OS) or [b]Command + Option + J[/b] (macOS). For Safari, first enable Developer Menu by going to Safari Menu > Preferences > Advanced tab and checking “Show Develop menu in menu bar,” then use the shortcut [b]Command + Option + C[/b]. For Firefox, the shortcut is [b]Control + Shift + K[/b] (Windows, Linux) or [b]Command + Option + K[/b] (macOS). For Microsoft Edge, the shortcut is [b]Control + Shift + J[/b]. 
[b]Step 3: Copy/paste the code into the console and hit Enter.[/b]
Once you do so, the code will prompt you in a popup, and you will not be able to access the tab you are running the code on. Do not switch to another tab in the same window, or the code will not work. 
[b]Step 4: Enter the username of the player you are ISOing. [/b]
This is case-sensitive; capitalize and spell the username correctly. 
[b]Step 5: Enter the first post of your ISO range.[/b]
If you are doing a full ISO, you can use 1 or 0. If you are doing a partial ISO, enter the post number from which you want the code to search for a player’s posts. 
[b]Step 6: Enter the last post of your ISO range.[/b]
If you are doing a full ISO, enter any number larger than the thread postcount; 10000 works. If you are doing a partial ISO, enter the post number to which you want the code to search for a player’s posts. Please note that posts that are within the range you have specified, but that have not been loaded in your browser, will not be included by the code in the ISO. 

Once you have done so, the reply field will open, with all the relevant posts quoted in it. [/hide]

Important notes: 
[list][*] The username is case sensitive. 
[*] The min/max limits of the range do not have to be exact. 
[*] The first range input must be smaller than the second range input. 
[*] This code does not work in [verbatim][pywindow][/verbatim].
[*] You cannot access the contents of the tab you are running the code in while it is running, so remember to memorize the target username before running the code. 
[*] You can rerun the code once it has finished running. 
[*] You can run the code even if you have a reply partly written. 
[*] If the code refuses to work, check that all of the above has been done correctly. If it still does not work, reload the tab and try again. [/list]

[b]For further troubleshooting, please PM the moderators of the game you are in.[/hide]

[b][s]Game is currently in progress. If you are not playing in this game, please do not post. You can go to [url=http://bit.ly/AMafSU]this thread[/url] to sign up.[/s][/b]

post_rendered:
D1/Dawn flavor hidden for convenience :)
The outcome of this game will influence the storylines of future UIC Invitationals.

“...”

“You’re late.”

A voice. Somewhat detached, spoken in an empty room. A statement, devoid of inflection or accusation.

“My apologies, your eminence. There was a slight delay in locating the Requisites, but everything is ready now.”

A beat. A hum.

“Such a lapse in planning is unbecoming of a man of your stature.” There was a small sound of amusement. “However, worry not. A few days matters not in the grand scheme of things. For time is one of the few things we still have in abundance, is it not?”

There was an answering laugh. It was an awkward laugh, the kind that one would make when one disagreed but couldn’t really say so. “Of course, of course. I am grateful for your magnanimity. It truly befits one who holds the temporal realm in the palm of a hand.”

“You flatter me.”

“But of course. In any case, our preparations are complete. The facilities are ready and the equipment is on standby, with trusted personnel there to assist. We await your move, your eminence.”

“Ah,” the other said. A rustle of clothes, and a couple of steps. “Then, let us begin.”

A blue light filled the room.

The universe groaned, an ancient machine built in aeons past protesting as it was drained of its temporality. Order across timelines across worlds, carefully wrought so it did not break, forced into contortions around this small pocket of space.

Nothing can stop time. It can be bent, reversed, twisted into a loop, but it must always flow. The most one can do is stretch it, slow it down so acutely that it may as well not be moving at all.

During one such infinitesimal moment, nine people vanished.

Dawn has started on 5/22/21 and will continue until you all confirm, there is a confirm rule

on dueling
Duels should be used as an extra hang, so we have double day pretty much.

Math says we have 4 mishangs till we lose.

7-2 gamestart
5-2 eod1
4-2 eon1
2-2 eod2

If we mishang twice today, then tomorrow is mylo, but we always hang because we have 2 hangs, so if we mishang once we'll just be in lylo.

Boatcounts for who should be hanged in duels, then the most consensus town duels that person so we don't have any disagreements on who should die in the duel and it ends quickly.

Each day phase is 14 days, so if we divide it, we should have one hang every week, so we have the duel after a week then the next hang after another week, but we don't have to follow this if we can't decide on a hang a week in.

But we always duel because it gives us more mishangs to spare.

DADV stands for Dead Air, Dead Villager. It's pretty much saying that if there's no opposition to a hang, the hang is villa. However, this is not DADV, and I will show how.

When you say that a wagon is DADV, you're also saying that there's no way the hang is scum. To disprove this for this scenario, let's assume msv is scum once.

We've already established that we always hang no matter what, so who do we hang? Well, most of the thread has been towny, and msv is by far the scummiest person. As a rule, we tend to hang the scummiest person, and msv is consensus the scummiest person, and thus, the best hang (we're going to ignore some recent stuff here).
Ok, so far, this post has been more a refutation of mj's arguments for msv town than why-is-mj-scummy. Well, I'll do that here.

The main point of the first section was to point out that mj's reasons for saying msv is town are bull.

Now that we've established that, let's move on. Let's look at mj's motivations for trying to prevent a msv hang.

Case 1: mj is town.

The only possible motivation for this case is that mj legitimately believes msv is town. He's provided his reasons for why, and I refuted them quite easily. Let's restate that in different words: Bob refuted mj quite easily. And since we seem to have a low opinion of my town play (rightfully), think over that for a second. Anyways, my point here is that mj's reasons were bull, I recognized that quite easily, and I find it hard to believe mj defended msv with such conviction without realizing his reasons were crap. And if he did, he would drop it. So I find this case hard to believe.

Case 2: mj is scum.

We have 2 possible motivations.

1. mj is scum trying to defend his buddy.

This is possible, but there are some reservations about this. As I have pointed out before in this post, scum defending msv (assuming msv is scum), is scummy, scum should know it, especially in this playerlist, and so this isn't very likely. However, I would think scum also knows this is WIFOM, and so it's still possible that they would defend msv like this.

2. mj is scum trying to defend town.

I find this the most likely. It's much less risk than trying to defend your buddy, as once msv flips town (remember, we're assuming msv is town here) it looks much less sus, and might even get some towncred. However, in this playerlist, I feel like scum would also recognize that people could look at this in hindsight and find it sus, but I think I could certainly play it off and so could other people.

So, while there are counterpoints against why all 3 scenarios aren't true, I find that both mj is scum scenarios are much more likely than mj being town.

Ok, so far, this post has been more a refutation of mj's arguments for msv town than why-is-mj-scummy. Well, I'll do that here.

The main point of the first section was to point out that mj's reasons for saying msv is town are bull.

Now that we've established that, let's move on. Let's look at mj's motivations for trying to prevent a msv hang.

Case 1: mj is town.

The only possible motivation for this case is that mj legitimately believes msv is town. He's provided his reasons for why, and I refuted them quite easily. Let's restate that in different words: Bob refuted mj quite easily. And since we seem to have a low opinion of my town play (rightfully), think over that for a second. Anyways, my point here is that mj's reasons were bull, I recognized that quite easily, and I find it hard to believe mj defended msv with such conviction without realizing his reasons were crap. And if he did, he would drop it. So I find this case hard to believe.

Case 2: mj is scum.

We have 2 possible motivations.

1. mj is scum trying to defend his buddy.

This is possible, but there are some reservations about this. As I have pointed out before in this post, scum defending msv (assuming msv is scum), is scummy, scum should know it, especially in this playerlist, and so this isn't very likely. However, I would think scum also knows this is WIFOM, and so it's still possible that they would defend msv like this.

2. mj is scum trying to defend town.

I find this the most likely. It's much less risk than trying to defend your buddy, as once msv flips town (remember, we're assuming msv is town here) it looks much less sus, and might even get some towncred. However, in this playerlist, I feel like scum would also recognize that people could look at this in hindsight and find it sus, but I think I could certainly play it off and so could other people.

So, while there are counterpoints against why all 3 scenarios aren't true, I find that both mj is scum scenarios are much more likely than mj being town.

In the traditional sense, duels are fought with deadly weapons, the deadlier the weapon, the better. One would be forgiven, then, for bringing a nuke to a duel.

BobDBuilder321 was one of those people, and this is the story of how it did not go very well for him.

Once upon a time, there was a nuke. This was no ordinary nuke, this was THE nuke. This nuke was the deadliest nuke ever created. It was also owned by Bob.

One day, Bob was bragging how he had the deadliest weapon in the world. mathjay claimed that having a nuke meant nothing since unlike their sword, Bob didn’t, and wouldn’t use it. There was a fight (in which threats of nuking people were frequently made), and so, the 2 people agreed on a duel using their weapons to see which one was superior.

It was obvious to Bob that his nuke was deadlier than anything else, so he walked into the duel with a plane carrying the nuke above the field. When the duel started, Bob ran away from mathjay(and their sword), shouting orders into his phone to launch the nuke.

It was launched. A few seconds later, everyone in the near vicinity was vaporized. Nothing left of them. It was a sad waste of a nuke.

mathjay’s close friends and family sued Bob’s company for killing them, and so, mathjay was revived free of cost. Why Bob was not revived, I do not know.

BobDBuilder321 was Mafia, the moral of the story is listen to your mum and not talk to strangers.

Bonus Flavor
Beep. Beep. Beep. Beep.

ad333 blearily blinked as he lifted his head from where he had pillowed it on his arms. He was sitting at the big table in the middle of what he and Nike100 had dubbed the control room, for lack of a more descriptive word. The rest of the room was covered in screens, from the walls to the holographic control panels wrapping around the room. Even the large tinted window that overlooked the main area of the facility, wherever they were, flickered with glowing status readouts and complicated-looking diagrams.

Right now, that window had been invaded by a horde of red words and exclamation marks. It was also evidently the source of all that beeping.

After a moment to shake himself awake, ad333 waved a hand in the air to grab the sensor’s attention, swiping it to the right. It stopped the beeping and dismissed the alerts crowding the observation window, just like he’d read in the manuals The Boss had tossed him before they were ushered here. Well. Usually, it would be figuratively, but this time he’d literally been tossed a strange device and he’d barely had time to ask one of the other unpaid interns for help figuring it out before he’d had to go.

He quickly scanned the screens around the room. All of them were showing the same group of people, but they were all slightly different. One of the screens on the left showed several shots of a burning wasteland, where a couple of people in hulking protective gear argued. Another cycled between a wide-angle view of what looked like a drifting interstellar vessel with its lights off, and a group of people muttering over a prone figure sprawled out on the floor in what appeared to be a military canteen. One section of the displays was even panning through what looked like the main offices of Unpaid Intern Central. It even had its own version of The Boss, yelling about something, probably a new product launch or missed sales goals or something worse. ad333 shuddered.

His eyes widened as he realized what had just happened. “Nike!” he called as he padded down the stairs. “Nike!” Under the control room, between the airlock leading to the main area and the hallway that led to the rest of the facility, there was another observation room. This one had some padded benches, and Nike had said earlier that she was looking to catch a quick nap there. They had been on standby for hours now, ever since the Requisites had begun arguing in all of the monitored instances. Waiting for something to happen.

And now one of them was dead.

Well, they weren’t actually, physically dead. Only in the “instances,” the shared simulation slash hallucinations that the Requisites were in. ad333 still wasn’t sure how it worked, or why this was so important, or even who the Requisites were. Right now, though, one of them had just been declared dead and they had to take care of the aftermath, just like they’d been instructed to.

The door to the observation room dinged pleasantly as he approached it, opening silently. The room behind it was dimmed, but he found Nike, who was curled up on one of the padded benches, leaning against the wall. She was wearing a pair of blue earphones, and her eyes were closed. ad333 gently shook her awake.

“One of the Requisites are dead,” he told her as she pulled out her earphones. “I dismissed the alert, but I didn’t send out the results yet. I wanted to wake you up first.”

Nike nodded, looking a lot more awake now that she’d heard the news. “Ah, thank you. I’ll go send out the results right now and report to Boss. You can handle taking the Requisite out of the pod by yourself, right?”

ad333 grimaced. “I think so. The instructions said that they’d be unconscious for a while even after they’re removed from the pod, so I should be fine. I’ll just use the stretcher.”

“Good luck,” Nike told him, getting up from the bench and stretching. “Give me one sec to wash up and I’ll go take care of the simulation. And tell Boss. Meet at the cafeteria after we’re both done?”

ad333 nodded briskly, and they both exited the room through opposite doorways.

* * * * *

The elevator doors opened with a soft chime and Nike stepped in. “Welcome,” it told her. Nike wasn’t quite sure whether it was male or female, but it sounded like most other artificial voices did, cool and calm and collected.

“Boss’s floor, please,” she replied to its silent question as the doors closed. The elevator, like the rest of the facility that she had seen, was made of something metallic and white. It was also utterly featureless; it didn’t even have a display indicating the floor number, or a panel of buttons. The only color it had were some blue, rectangular patterns that faded in and out around the doors.

After a couple seconds of moving up, Nike could feel the elevator slow to a stop, and the doors opened again, this time onto a floor that looked a lot more normal. It actually looked like the floor of a hotel, actually, albeit still minimalistic. The lighting was slightly dimmer, with tiled sections of the wall glowing a cool white, and the light grey carpet she was walking on was soft and thick. It was also really quiet.

She stopped in front of a door - she knew it was the right one because it was the only one on this floor that had blue lines glowing on its surface - and she knocked. The door reacted, its lights flickering, and she could hear a voice talking on the other side. “Sir, Nike100 is here to see you.”

“Come in!” came the answering yell, and the door slid open to reveal The Boss. Well, her universe’s version of The Boss, as she’d come to learn. He was currently dressed in a formal suit that was bright purple and appeared to have a lot of small, neon-green aliens dancing across it. Nike did her best to pretend nothing was out of the ordinary.

“Boss, we’ve just had our first instance termination. One of the Requisites assigned the alignment of “Mafia” was just beaten to death in at least four of the instances. ad333 is currently transferring the subject from the pod to their assigned chambers, and the recordings of the terminated instance are currently being compiled and organized for analysis.”

Boss frowned. “Say again?”

“We have just had our first instance termination, and-”

“No no no, not that part,” Boss said, interrupting her. “The Mafia part. Did you say a Mafia died?”

“Yes, Boss,” Nike replied, holding onto her poker face for dear life.

“Aw, dang,” he said.

There was a pause.

“That is indeed terrible news,” he said mournfully. “And not the good kind of horrible either, like Paid Intern Central’s atrociously pitiable earnings reports for last quarter. Have you seen them?”

When Nike shook her head, he snapped his fingers, bringing up a concerningly large spreadsheet full of numbers and numbers and even more numbers. The holographic display was almost as tall as he was and the spreadsheet was still scrolling, digits in red and green and purple flicking past too fast for her to read. “Simply terrible, isn’t it?” He snapped his fingers again and the hologram disappeared in a flicker of pixels. “But we’re getting off track! Back to terrible news. Hmm.”

He gave a disappointed puff. “There’s nothing we can do about it. And this’ll end up delaying this project by a week or two, maybe more if we’re unlucky. I wonder if there’s a way to sell luck? I should ask R&D if they ever finished analyzing that overpriced luck potion I snatched in, what was it. Yaerial? Vaeryal? Fun country.” He trailed off.

“It’s okay though, don't worry,” he said, turning back to Nike. “This totally won’t derail any of our plans or destroy the world or anything. Fear not, for your job security isn’t in danger and your access to our regularly scheduled programming of back-breaking and horrifically mundane work weeks is forever guaranteed! Limited terms and conditions apply, of course,” he said, coughing pointedly into his fist. “Now, pardon me while I speedwalk to an entirely unrelated and unimportant emergency meeting that I felt the need to schedule right now!”

So saying, he left, leaving Nike behind his office.

post_rendered:
here's all of the death flavor expertly formatted and tweaked around the events of the game and not just copy-pasted with extra hide tags / a few minor edits I take no responsibility for anything in here, particularly in regards to the continuity or the lack thereof
mathjay deaths
Hang, town/scum
"IT'S YOU, ISN'T IT?"

The door to the small cabin slid open with a hiss - which wasn't quite as dramatic as it would've been if a physical door had been slammed open - but mathjay still flinched from where he’d been sitting on the floor, stretching.

He looked up to see his crewmates storm through the door, scowls on their faces. A few of them were hanging back behind the others, just watching, but most of them were carrying something. A metal bar, a blaster, synthetic fiber cables. One of them was even carrying a portable welding unit, and mathjay’s eyes widened.

“It’s not me,” he protested, scrambling to his feet and holding his hands up. He backed away, looking warily at the blaster being pointed directly in his face. “I told you before, I don’t have the technical expertise to hijack the system. I couldn’t have messed with it even if I wanted to!”

“Bull*Censored because I don't know who will see this*,” sneered the one who was carrying the blaster. “How dumb do you think we are? We all know that this entire operation was meticulously planned, and it was only dumb luck that seven of us weren’t gassed along with everyone else. It doesn’t matter that you can’t hack the system yourself because you could’ve been given a device to do it for you!”

“Or your partner could’ve done it as well!” someone else yelled.

“Guys, it’s still not me,” mathjay said weakly. He was backed up against the wall at this point. “I’m just hired security. I had nothing to do with any of this, I swear.”

“It doesn't matter,” the other answered. “Everyone else who could've done it is already dead. You should've seen this coming."

"You just said anyone could've done it!"

"For your crimes against the Interstellar Commonwealth and your involvement in the Vespermoon plot, mathjay, you are hereby sentenced to execution.”

"You can't do th-"

There was the sharp crack of a blaster discharge, and then a small thump.
NK, Town
"You know, I've done this too many times to count, but this really never gets old."

A figure, dressed in a midnight blue hood and barely visible in the moonlight, curled his lip in distaste. "I think you're enjoying this too much. You should really remember that we're only doing this for our sovereignty."

The other snorted. "Oh pish posh, why does that matter?" His hair was fiery red, his clothes were embroidered with silver stitching, and he didn't even bother trying to sneak around. He idly scuffed his shoes on the dirt as he spoke. "Killing is killing and death is death. If the world suddenly goes mad and decides to glorify what used to be a crime, who am I to question it?"

"You are such a barbarian," the one in the hood scoffed. They were standing in the shadow of a grove of trees, looking out over a small camp. A couple of tents had been pitched, grouped around the remains of a now-extinguished campfire, and the horses and wagons had been tied up nearby.

Foolishly, the travelers did not think to take turns keeping watch. Not that it would've helped much, but it made assassination too easy.

The redhead yawned, revealing his point incisors, and flicked his elven ears in annoyance. "It's late," he said curtly. "We should get this over with. Who do we kill tonight?"

His companion grimaced. "That one," he said, pointing at a particularly scuffed-looking tent. "I would really rather not, and instead trick the humans into killing themselves off, but we really need to-"

"Don't worry about it," redhead said, cutting the other off. "No need to get your hands all dirty, I got this." So saying, he stalked off towards said tent.

Scipio1 deaths
Hang:
“WHAT”
“WHAT”
“WHAT”
Scipio1 looked in shock as he saw the ReaperBotTM’s message:

“Congratulations, Scipio1, your reap earned 53769 points.You also got a 7x reap!

The game has ended!
The winner is Scipio1!”

Scipio1 was unable to contain his amazement as he saw that he had just won the game with one single reap. That amazement slowly began to turn into excitement when he realized that he was one step closer to getting a raise from TheBossTM, and he had done it without the hours of effort usually required.

But when the others found out, they didn’t congratulate him for winning, or express their shock that such a thing could happen; instead, they seemed… angry.

“That’s impossible!”
“It shouldn’t count!”

“He probably cheated!”

That was all it took. Suddenly, all the others nodded and looked at him in suspicion that he had somehow manipulated the ReaperBotTM in his favor. Before Scipio1 even got a chance to try to explain himself, the others forced him out… out… and away...

Scipio1 has been exiled by an angry mob after winning a suspiciously fast game of Reaper. He was a _____.

NK:
Ticinus. Trebia. Zama. All these battles, and somehow, Scipio1 had managed to survive, and even come out on top. But what he never could have imagined is that his ultimate death would come from one of his own, one of his fellow soldiers. Not Hannibal or another Carthiginian, one of his own. A traitor.

As it turns out, years of training and preparation as a general don’t help when you’re attacked in your sleep by one of your own.

Scipio1 was killed. He was a ___________.

EulerNTM death
EulerNTM has procrastinated and has forgotten to write a good death flavor, so he was left behind trying to quickly come up with one while everyone else left him behind. He was ___. - I’ll finish this later no


BobDBuilder321’s Death
Duel
In the traditional sense, duels are fought with deadly weapons, the deadlier the weapon, the better. One would be forgiven, then, for bringing a nuke to a duel.

BobDBuilder321 was one of those people, and this is the story of how it did not go very well for him.

Once upon a time, there was a nuke. This was no ordinary nuke, this was THE nuke. This nuke was the deadliest nuke ever created. It was also owned by Bob.

One day, Bob was bragging how he had the deadliest weapon in the world. [Other Duelist] claimed that having a nuke meant nothing since unlike their sword, Bob didn’t, and wouldn’t use it. There was a fight (in which threats of nuking people were frequently made), and so, the 2 people agreed on a duel using their weapons to see which one was superior.

It was obvious to Bob that his nuke was deadlier than anything else, so he walked into the duel with a plane carrying the nuke above the field. When the duel started, Bob ran away from {Other Duelist] (and their sword), shouting orders into his phone to launch the nuke.

It was launched. A few seconds later, everyone in the near vicinity was vaporized. Nothing left of them. It was a sad waste of a nuke.

[Other Duelist]’s close friends and family sued Bob’s company for killing them, and so, [Other Duelist] was revived free of cost. Why Bob was not revived, I do not know.

BobDBuilder321 was a [flip], the moral of the story is listen to your mum and not talk to strangers.

Hang
BobDBuilder321 struggled against the ropes binding him, protests muffled by the greasy rag in his mouth.

Ceremonial hanging, of course, he thought. They couldn’t make this quick and easy.

His knife was in his pocket, a last minute desperate hope before he was seized. More annoyance than hope though; his hands were tied and the knife was poking uncomfortably against his side.

He was supposed to be hanged at sundown, and the sky was already turning shades of red and yellow. Much like me, I assume. Not much longer till death.

Not many people fear death, but dying, that they do. And so, as thoughts of a sword cleaving through his head made their way into his mind, Bob started struggling more frantically. He didn’t have plans for what he’d do if he escaped, nothing for what he wanted to do either, but he did know that he didn’t want to die.

I don’t want to die. And with that, his ropes fell off, like a magician performing his latest escape trick. Bob was up in an instant, running away, the rag left ripped in the dirt.

Looking behind him, Bob saw no one in pursuit, and an exhausted whoop found its way through his lips. He kept running though, running and running as far away as he could from that wretched, wretched place.

Then he tripped. Fell on his side. His knife pierced through skin and flesh, blood burst from the wound. And there he lay, bleeding out, dying. Eyelids closed, breathing slowed, and there he lies dead.

BobDBuilder321 was a [flip], his ghost is very .

NK (Also this may be somewhat triggering to some people)
BobDBuilder321 wakes up to find himself tied to a chair, gagging on a cloth. He shuts his eyes quickly, but not soon enough.

“Good, you’re awake! Let’s start!” says a voice. Bob recognizes it.

“You?” he tries to say, gagging again. He struggles, trying to get out of his bindings.

“Nuh uh, we can’t have you leaving so soon, can we?”

Pain explodes inside his head, and then, his sight is gone. Both eyes gouged out fast. Muffled screaming is heard.

Bob notices the lack of sensation in his fingers, his hand, and then his arm. He blacks out.

In the morning, Bob is found dismembered, only identified because of the lack of Bob anywhere else. Dried blood stains the floor, random limbs are strewn over the place, and a variety of colors stain the ceiling.

Many walk out immediately, speak nothing, do nothing, feel nothing but the vomit rising in their throats.

BobDBuilder321 was a Vanilla Townie, may his pieces rest in peace.

snoozingnewt death
Hang/Duel (Town)
Snoozingnewt realized that this game, he could vent about town possibly being oh-so-dumb and wrong in his mishang flavor flip.
Snoozingnewt’s coworkers were salty at snoozingnewt’s brilliant excellence and so decided to murder him because of their jealousy, even though he was completely and utterly innocent. Yes, snoozingnewt was innocent. You are buffoons if you are reading this. Go eat a sock.
Snoozingnewt was fantasizing about roasting his coworkers after he got resurrected. He would gloat in their faces about how they were wrong and about how they are all bad at logic games. He would be angrier than he was in 215.
“GRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRR.” snoozingnewt’s ghost said as he tried and failed to bonk hammerer’s/last voter head with a hammer. And I think the town deserves to lose after hanging me anyway. Scum ggwp. He then tried to bonk his main pusher with a hammer, and it worked.

“OwWWWWWwwWWW!11!”

Snoozingnewt felt a little better.



Hang/Duel (Scum)
It’s 3p LyLo, isn’t it?
Yes, that’s it in terms of flavor. That’s literally it. Don’t bug me for more.

NK
BossTM has caught snoozingnewt snoozing on the job instead of writing flavor like a true ProcrastinatorTM, and has called death to NightReapTM him.
It wasn’t a normal morning at UnpaidInternCentralTM, sure, there was snoozingnewt mysteriously missing and there was blood all over the place, but most importantly, TheOneAndOnlyTM ReaperBotTM was actually up for more than two days on end without crashing. This was distressing, because no one was getting any sleep anymore due to the uptime. Someone called 911 about the bot. “Hello, 911 emergency, we have a weird bot that actually works! Oh, and let me add I think someone got murdered but that’s not very important.”

The response on the other end was even more concerning.

“Which bot? Is it the reaper bot where you say ‘reap’ to get points?”

Ding! 911_Operator has gotten a 13x reap and has won the game with 839184 points.

“D:<” was the approximate reaction of person. They smashed the phone and its splintering pieces fizzled in a spilled bottle of aloe juice.

“I bet someone actually made the bot work for once! Whoever did this must be executed!” As everyone started pointing fingers at each other, one of whom which had a shirt soaked with blood (but no one noticed because robots don’t have blood), the room grew ever more tense as the remaining hour of sleep from the bot not working at 3 AMTM in the morning slowly disappeared.

There was a fight (in which threats of nuking people were frequently made), and so, the 2 people agreed on a duel using their weapons to see which one was superior.

It was obvious to Bob that his nuke was deadlier than anything else, so he walked into the duel with a plane carrying the nuke above the field. When the duel started, Bob ran away from {Other Duelist] (and their sword), shouting orders into his phone to launch the nuke.

It was launched. A few seconds later, everyone in the near vicinity was vaporized. Nothing left of them. It was a sad waste of a nuke.

[Other Duelist]’s close friends and family sued Bob’s company for killing them, and so, [Other Duelist] was revived free of cost. Why Bob was not revived, I do not know.

BobDBuilder321 was a [flip], the moral of the story is listen to your mum and not talk to strangers.
[/hide]
[hide=Hang]
BobDBuilder321 struggled against the ropes binding him, protests muffled by the greasy rag in his mouth. 

Ceremonial hanging, of course, he thought. They couldn’t make this quick and easy. 

His knife was in his pocket, a last minute desperate hope before he was seized. More annoyance than hope though; his hands were tied and the knife was poking uncomfortably against his side.

He was supposed to be hanged at sundown, and the sky was already turning shades of red and yellow. Much like me, I assume. Not much longer till death. 

Not many people fear death, but dying, that they do. And so, as thoughts of a sword cleaving through his head made their way into his mind, Bob started struggling more frantically. He didn’t have plans for what he’d do if he escaped, nothing for what he wanted to do either, but he did know that he didn’t want to die.

I don’t want to die. And with that, his ropes fell off, like a magician performing his latest escape trick. Bob was up in an instant, running away, the rag left ripped in the dirt.

Looking behind him, Bob saw no one in pursuit, and an exhausted whoop found its way through his lips. He kept running though, running and running as far away as he could from that wretched, wretched place.

Then he tripped. Fell on his side. His knife pierced through skin and flesh, blood burst from the wound. And there he lay, bleeding out, dying. Eyelids closed, breathing slowed, and there he lies dead.

BobDBuilder321 was a [flip], his ghost is very :mad:.
[/hide]
[hide=NK (Also this may be somewhat triggering to some people)]
BobDBuilder321 wakes up to find himself tied to a chair, gagging on a cloth. He shuts his eyes quickly, but not soon enough.

“Good, you’re awake! Let’s start!” says a voice. Bob recognizes it.

“You?” he tries to say, gagging again. He struggles, trying to get out of his bindings.

“Nuh uh, we can’t have you leaving so soon, can we?”

Pain explodes inside his head, and then, his sight is gone. Both eyes gouged out fast. Muffled screaming is heard.

Bob notices the lack of sensation in his fingers, his hand, and then his arm. He blacks out.

In the morning, Bob is found dismembered, only identified because of the lack of Bob anywhere else. Dried blood stains the floor, random limbs are strewn over the place, and a variety of colors stain the ceiling. 

Many walk out immediately, speak nothing, do nothing, feel nothing but the vomit rising in their throats.

BobDBuilder321 was a Vanilla Townie, may his pieces rest in peace.
